mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation expectations, as indicated by a 3-month increase in CPIAUCSL above 2.5%, prompt central banks to tighten monetary policy, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals strong aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: Joint growth in GDPC1 and HOUST fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising FEDFUNDS combined with elevated CPIAUCSL creates stagflationary risks, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where both FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL > 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 100k jobs per month signals robust labor market conditions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through increased consumer spending.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) below its 12-month moving average indicates weakening supply-side capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through reduced corporate profitability.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO falls below its 12-month MA.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS) above 350 billion dollars signals strong demand-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through sustained corporate revenue growth.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 40% of instances where RSAFS exceeds 350B.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) indicate deteriorating supply-side conditions and labor market weakness, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO < 100 and UNRATE > 4.5%.
